theme: snowplows clearing a city where cleared streets speed everyone up
title: Blizzard Fleet Bootstrap
seed_problem: multi-vehicle arc routing (Chinese postman with a fleet)
mutation: composite: single-tour arc routing is generalized to a K-vehicle split (fleet-partition) whose edge traversal cost is state-dependent (self-accelerating-network: the first, plow-mode, traversal of a street is slow but permanently unlocks fast traversal for every future crew), coupling the vehicle-to-work assignment to a network-wide investment decision.
strategies: trivial: one crew clears the whole city alone in arbitrary (input) order via a DFS double-cover, the other crews idle (reproduces the checker baseline)
naive greedy: after crossing the shared trunk street, split the city's regions across crews round-robin by nearest-spoke order, ignoring how much local work each region actually contains

insight (strong): find the bridge edges (the backbone/trunk+spokes) generically, clear them cooperatively first so every crew gets fast repositioning, then assign whole regions to crews by total workload (longest-processing-time bin-packing) to balance finish times
a general solver can go further: dynamically re-balance mid-route, split a single region between two crews, or route so multiple crews share partial prefixes of the backbone
